example_sentence: Es caro; sin embargo, vale la pena.
example_translation: It's expensive; however, it's worth it.
She speaks English; furthermore, she knows French.

example_sentence: No estudió; por lo tanto, reprobó.
example_translation: He didn't study; therefore, he failed.

example_sentence: Se me hizo tarde, así que tomé un taxi.
example_translation: I was running late, so I took a cab.

example_sentence: Ya que estás aquí, ayúdame con esto.
example_translation: Since you're here, help me with this.

translation: despite / in spite of (the fact that)
example_sentence: Fue al trabajo a pesar de estar enfermo.
example_translation: He went to work despite being sick.

part_of_speech: conjunction (contrast — used after negation to replace/correct)
example_translation: I don't want water but rather juice.

translation: in other words / I mean / that is to say
example_sentence: O sea, no entiendo lo que quieres decir.
example_translation: I mean, I don't understand what you're trying to say.

word: por un lado... por otro lado
translation: on one hand... on the other hand
pronunciation_hint: pohr oon LAH-doh... pohr OH-troh LAH-doh
example_sentence: Por un lado me gusta el trabajo; por otro lado, el salario es bajo.
example_translation: On one hand I like the job; on the other hand, the salary is low.

example_sentence: No llegó; es más, ni mandó un mensaje.
example_translation: He didn't show up; what's more, he didn't even send a message.

translation: on the other hand / instead / whereas
example_sentence: A mí me gusta el picante; mi hermana, en cambio, no tolera el chile.
example_translation: I like spicy food; my sister, on the other hand, can't handle chili.

example_sentence: Si bien el proceso es largo, los resultados valen la pena.
example_translation: While the process is long, the results are worth it.
